Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Continued To Apr-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-22-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for and with Gavin Morrison by zoom.
Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for Institute on Ageing.
Carrie Moulton (Director of Fiduciary Services of Institute on Ageing) appeared by zoom.
John Kithas appeared by zoom for and with Judith Brisby by zoom and for Bonnie Morrison.

The Court noted that the parties have identified a neutral third party and thanked them. The Court also noted that there are no objections to the petition from the Institute on Ageing and no objection to admission of the will. The Court will grant the appointment of Institute on Ageing as the Administrator of the estate with full authority and bond set $465,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for March 15, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed filed by Gavin Morrison is off calendar. Ms. Navia stated that she has no objection to Mr. Morrison's petition going off calendar. All Parties indicated that they have nothing to add. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed filed by Institute on Ageing is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-15-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Continued To Aug-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for Gavin Morrison (Petitioner/Beneficiary).
John Kithas appeared by zoom for Judith Brisby.

Mr. Kithas stated that there was an objection to Mr. Morrison's petition and that they had an agreement to appoint Carrie Molten. Ms. Navia stated that Ms. Alavi represents Carrie Molten of the Institute on Aging, that she has no objection to Ms. Molten serving as the Administrator and that Ms. Alavi indicated that she will be filing her petition by the end of this week. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to August 18, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jul-07-2020 Continued To Aug-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-07-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Gavin Morrison.
John Kithas appeared by telephone for and with Judith Brisby (by telephone) and for and with Bonie Morrison (by telephone).
Paula Bibbs (Proposed Neutral) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that the will names Bonnie and then Judith as the Executors, that Bonnie declined to act and that both parties are seeking to have the will admitted. Ms. Navia stated that she amended her petition to have any neutral fiduciary appointed and that her Client is willing to accept that the will be probated, but not have Ms. Brisby as the Executor. Discussion was held regarding whether there are grounds for removal, circumstances with the house, whether there was undue influence at the time of execution of the will, the urgency for Ms. Brisby and Ms. Morrison's actions to enter into the contracts regarding sale of the house, the contracts being entered into with no authority, the property being in foreclosure, the value of the property used in signing said contracts, objections to having Ms. Bibbs appointed, the amount of care and support Ms. Brisby and Ms. Morrison provided to the Decedent, etc. Ms. Navia spoke of Ms. Bibbs qualifications and professional standing in the community, stated that Ms. Bibbs would not be represented by her firm, that Ms. Bibbs and Mr. Kithas can speak as to what she would be doing for the estate and that if Mr. Kithas does not want to have Ms. Bibbs serve, she will leave it up to him to suggest someone. The Court rules that the actions of Ms. Bisby entering into the contracts without any authority are grounds for removal and that the subject of the contracts may be the subject of litigation. Ms. Brisby's Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is denied. Mr. Morrison's Amended Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration is continued to August 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to select and agree on a neutral fiduciary.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Reporter: Lucy Carrillo-Grubbs (by telephone), CSR #6766, 415-597-5600, lucycsr@comcast.net

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jul-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jul-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Continued To Apr-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Kithas for and with Judith Brisby.
Rowena Navia for Gavin Morrison (Petitioner/Heir).

The Court noted that there are competing petitions, that no will contest has been filed and asked where things stand. Mr. Kithas stated that they exchanged e-mails, that mediation did not work and that they need to determine if Ms. Navia's Client is contesting the will, or just against the appointment of his Client. Ms. Navia stated that her Client wishes for leave to amend his petition for appointment of an Administrator with will annexed, that her Client wants a private professional to serve and that her Client's primary concern is to have a neutral party. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed, and the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary are continued to April 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Navia's petition is to be filed with enough time for notice and is to be set for April 14, 2020.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Continued To Apr-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Continued To Mar-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Kithas for and with Judith Brisby (Petitioner).
Rowena Navia for and with Gavin Morrison (Petitioner).
Paula Bibbs (nominated Professional) also present.

The Court noted that no will contest has been filed and that it needs to happen so that a summons can be issued. Ms. Navia suggested that the Parties agree to having a neutral Professional in place, stated that she would rather have a neutral person, that when the Decedent became ill he only told two people, that in 2018 and 2019 the Decedent was in the process of refinancing and the property went into foreclosure, that as the Decedent got worse he was unable to keep up with things and was receiving help from Ms. Dumont, and that there is an issue of capacity. Mr. Kithas stated that he does not think having a Professional is necessary and that it will create additional costs and expense to the estate. Ms. Navia stated that there is a real property that is either rented, or there is some kind of purchase agreement out there, that they don't know the status of the real property, that Ms. Brisby tried to have her Client sign a $29,000.00 promissory note and that they don't know anything about the personal property. Ms. Navia also stated that someone needs to be in place, that she would like to settle this with having a Neutral and that there is a legitimate question as to the validity of the will. The Court noted that the Parties have created a mess and that they have the ability to get out of this, but not by digging in their hills, that all of this fighting is going to create expense and delay of the estate, and that they hold the key to streamlining this. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 28, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 4, 2020. The Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary are continued to and the Parties are to return on March 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. If there is no agreement, the Parties are to provide a joint status report by March 4, 2020 that shows that they have met and conferred and where things will go from here. The Court strongly encourages the Parties to reach an agreement.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
